I watch few tv shows once in a while. It’s the first time I see a story like this. A Love story of 2 black teens facing teenager challenges. I felt really happy and hope my future kids will see people like them on TV. While growing up, I did never felt appreciated by a black man my age.  Some of them would like me  but only for sex which I never did. Some also liked me in secret, so their friends wouldn’t know. I was mostly being chased by older men which I avoided at all costs. I had a very good first love with a white boy. Black boys mostly had eyes for white girls my age. I’m not playing the victim or resenting but I think black men should learn also to love their equals.

I'm an old man who grew up on recording the quiet storm to cassette tapes. It seemed like media was boycotting the black love story, because the narratives of who we are seems to be so intentionally negative. I Love this show, the way it was written, and the fact that it's so relatable to me, my family, and very similar scenarios that have taken place over our journey. I was happy to see that Regina King, and so many others, had a hand in directing this beautiful piece of hyperrealism. Thank you, and I hope there's more to come. We are so much more, and the diaspora is a beautiful Love story. Great job to the cast and crew 👏👏👏💪🏿✊🏿👍🏿✌️🙏🏿🙌🏿💯

Whilst the drama's premise is beautiful, the comments are disheartening and disappointing. We're in 2025 and yet people are still whining about there's not enough black this or that out there. Spike Lee overcame immense challenges to make She's Gotta Have It (a story incidentally about black love), and similarly black film makers before him. Now technology is so pervasive and accessible, you have no excuse but to go out and make you're own stories if you don't see yourself on screen.

Some candidates from the last 30 or so years.

Awkward Black Girl

Entergalactic

Poetic Justice

Love Jones (1997)

Love & Basketball (2000)

If Beale Street Could Talk (2018) 

Rye Lane (2023) - 

Premature (2019)

ATL 2006

The Wood (1999)

Dope (2015)

She's Gotta Have It

A Love Song for Latasha

My Fault 2025

Sylvie's Love 2020
